findings.title: Misspellings in title
findings.snippet: On the Convergance of Iteratve Methods
findings.explanation: The title contains two misspellings: 'Convergance' and 'Iteratve'. These are immediately visible and undermine credibility.
findings.fix: On the Convergence of Iterative Methods

findings.title: Multiple misspellings in first sentence
findings.snippet: This paper studys the convergance of iterative methods for solving linar systems.
findings.explanation: 'studys' should be 'studies', 'convergance' should be 'convergence', and 'linar' should be 'linear'.
findings.fix: This paper studies the convergence of iterative methods for solving linear systems.

findings.title: Misspelling: 'importent'
findings.snippet: The importent contribution of this work
findings.explanation: 'importent' is misspelled. This error recurs in the Conclusion section as well.
findings.fix: The important contribution of this work

findings.title: Subject–verb disagreement: 'we provides'
findings.snippet: we provides a new bound on the convergence rate
findings.explanation: The subject 'we' requires the plural verb form 'provide'.
findings.fix: we provide a new bound on the convergence rate

findings.title: Multiple agreement errors in one sentence
findings.snippet: Previous work have shown that such methods converges under certain conditions,
findings.explanation: 'Previous work' (uncountable) takes a singular verb 'has shown', and 'methods' (plural) takes the plural verb 'converge'.
findings.fix: Previous work has shown that such methods converge under certain conditions,

findings.title: Double comparative and agreement errors
findings.snippet: but our result are more tighter and applies to a broader class of problems.
findings.explanation: Three problems: (1) 'result' should be 'results' (or keep singular with 'is'); (2) 'more tighter' is a double comparative—use 'tighter'; (3) 'applies' should be 'apply' to agree with the plural subject.
findings.fix: but our results are tighter and apply to a broader class of problems.

findings.title: Wrong article: 'a $n \times n$'
findings.explanation: The word 'n' begins with a vowel sound, so the article should be 'an'.

findings.title: Subject–verb disagreement: 'eigenvalues is'
findings.snippet: which means that all its eigenvalues is positive.
findings.fix: which means that all its eigenvalues are positive.

findings.title: Possessive 'its' written as contraction 'it's'
findings.snippet: the largest absolute value of it's eigenvalues.
findings.explanation: 'it's' is the contraction of 'it is'. The possessive form is 'its'.
findings.fix: the largest absolute value of its eigenvalues.

findings.title: Wrong article: 'a iterative method'
findings.snippet: We recall that a iterative method of the form
findings.explanation: 'iterative' begins with a vowel sound; the article should be 'an'.
findings.fix: We recall that an iterative method of the form

findings.title: Wrong participle: 'have showed'
findings.snippet: We have showed that the Jacobi method converges
findings.explanation: The past participle of 'show' is 'shown', not 'showed'.
findings.fix: We have shown that the Jacobi method converges

findings.title: Subject–verb disagreement: 'bound … are sharp'
findings.snippet: The bound we derived are sharp and cannot be improved
findings.fix: The bound we derived is sharp and cannot be improved

findings.title: Repeated misspelling: 'importent' in Conclusion
findings.snippet: This result has importent implications for practical computations.
findings.explanation: Same misspelling as in the Introduction; should be 'important'.
findings.fix: This result has important implications for practical computations.
